login.title: Sign in to your workspace
login.failed: Sign-in failed. Check your username and password.

portal.title: My workspaces
portal.empty: You have no workspace yet. Ask your administrator to create one for you.
portal.name: Name (optional)
portal.openWhereHint: You can change this anytime from your profile.
portal.deleteConfirm: Delete this workspace? This cannot be undone from the portal.
portal.noTunableParams: This protocol has no user-tunable parameters on this template.
portal.protocolLockedHint: This template does not allow overriding the protocol.
portal.loadError: Your workspaces could not be loaded. Check your connection and try again.
portal.templateUnavailable: unavailable (image not allowed by your policy)
portal.advancedModeHint: Deviations from the template, limited to what your policy and the template allow. Every override is recorded in the audit trail.
portal.fixedSizing: Sizing set by the template: {{cpu}} vCPU, {{memory}} (your policy does not allow custom sizing)

events.title: Events — {{name}}
events.empty: No recent events for this workspace.

drift.full: This workspace's configuration changed (template update or your own settings). The desktop will restart with the new version the next time it resumes — or now, by clicking this badge.
drift.why: The configuration of this workspace changed: its template was updated, or you changed its settings.
drift.when: It applies automatically at the next stop/start (pause/resume or scheduled downtime).
drift.how: Click to restart the desktop and apply it now.
drift.reloadConfirm: Restart the desktop now to apply the pending changes? Unsaved work in the current session will be lost.

runtime.hint: Changes to this workspace apply at its next stop/start; until then the card shows an "update pending" badge you can click to restart immediately.
runtime.locked: not allowed by this template or your policy

remote.hint: Machines outside the cluster you connect to through the gateway (SSH, VNC or RDP). Nothing is provisioned: you manage these machines yourself.
remote.loadError: Your remote workspaces could not be loaded.
remote.name: Name
remote.credentialsHint: Stored server-side in an encrypted Secret, never shown again. On edit, leave a field blank to keep the stored value.
remote.noCredentials: No credentials stored — the target will prompt you.
remote.deleteConfirm: Remove this remote workspace? Its stored credentials will be deleted. The machine itself is not touched.
remote.macHint: Optional. Enables powering the machine on via the platform relay. WoL only works if the relay is on the same L2 network as the target.
remote.wakeHint: Send a Wake-on-LAN magic packet via the platform relay.
remote.wakeWaitHint: Sent a Wake-on-LAN packet; giving the machine time to boot before retrying.

connect.failed: Could not connect to the desktop.
connect.wakeHint: This can take a moment while the workspace scales back up.
connect.wakeTimeout: “{{name}}” did not become ready in time. It may still be starting — retry, or check its status from the portal.

splitView.hint: Up to {{max}} workspaces side by side — hover a pane to split, swap or close it.
splitView.pickWorkspace: Choose a workspace for this pane
splitView.noRunning: No running workspace. Start one from the portal first.

profile.title: My profile
profile.oidcNote: These fields will be managed by your identity provider once SSO is enabled.
profile.openTargetUnset: Not set yet — you will be asked on first open.

templatesPage.name: Name
templatesPage.protocolsHint: Each protocol maps to guacd; parameters come from the platform registry. Check "user-adjustable" to let workspace users override a parameter.
templatesPage.envHint: Literal values or Secret references — secrets must come from a Secret, never inline.
templatesPage.overridesHint: Fields workspace creators may deviate from this template (admins may always override everything).
templatesPage.workloadHint: CR workload block as JSON: kind, security contexts, volumes, nodeSelector, tolerations, serviceAccountName.
templatesPage.placementHint: Empty = the platform-wide pattern (operator env), then the built-in "waas-workspace". Only NEW workspaces follow a change.
templatesPage.placeholdersTitle: Available placeholders (each value is sanitized to DNS-1123):
templatesPage.noProtocolsYet: No protocol configured yet: add the ones this template must serve (without a protocols block, the legacy OS-derived entry applies).

usersPage.groupsHint: Comma-separated IdP (OIDC) group names. Overwritten at each SSO login when OIDC is enabled.
usersPage.noPolicy: no policy matches — workspace creation is denied
usersPage.createGroupsHint: Pick from known groups or add a name. No group = the user matches only the default policy. Overwritten at first SSO login when OIDC is enabled.

governance.invalidYaml: Invalid YAML — fix the highlighted lines first
governance.gitopsNote: Changes here edit the cluster CRDs directly; Git only seeds them (ArgoCD selfHeal must stay off for these kinds).
governance.name: Name
governance.invalidName: Name must be a lowercase DNS-1123 label (a-z, 0-9, -).

overlay.title: Session menu
overlay.reconnectHint: These parameters are fixed at connect time by guacd: applying reconnects the session.
overlay.switchConfirm: Switch to {{protocol}}? The current session will reconnect.
overlay.switchHint: Switching reconnects the session with the selected protocol.
overlay.clipboardReceivedEmpty: Nothing received yet — copy some text inside the workspace.
overlay.clipboardSendPlaceholder: Text to send to the workspace clipboard…
overlay.clipboardInsecureHint: Without HTTPS the browser blocks automatic sync: use Ctrl+V inside the session to paste, and this manual exchange to fetch a copy.

schedule.title: Schedule (uptime / downtime)
schedule.hint: Planned start/stop by cron (standard 5-field), in an explicit timezone. Downtime scales the workspace to 0; a manual action wins until the next opposite scheduled edge.

protocolTabs.removeConfirm: Remove the {{protocol}} protocol and its parameters?
protocolTabs.lastProtocol: The last protocol cannot be removed

volumes.name: Volume
volumes.empty: No retained volume. When deleting a workspace you may keep its volume: it will show up here.
volumes.quotaNote: Retained volumes count against your storage quota for as long as they exist.
volumes.deleteWarning: Volume {{volume}}{{size}} will be deleted with the workspace unless you keep it.
volumes.keepChoiceHint: The volume stays yours (Volumes tab) and keeps counting against your storage quota. It can be reattached to a new workspace.
volumes.deleteChoiceHint: The home data will be permanently lost.
volumes.deleteVolumeConfirm: Permanently delete volume {{name}} and its data?
volumes.attachHint: Retained volumes compatible with the destination namespace.
